blocks.description: Full-bleed hero teaser with background image (or video on homepage), heading overlay, and optional CTA button. Source site uses AEM teaser component (class: hero-teaser, emu-teaser) with desktop/mobile image variants.
blocks.notes: Homepage hero includes auto-playing background video. Treatment and FAQ pages use static hero images. All heroes have h1 text overlay. Clinic-finder hero has a distinct style class (juvederm-findaclinic-hero) but same pattern.

blocks.description: Expandable question/answer sections. FAQ page has 5 accordion groups (About Juvéderm, Preparation, Consultation, Results, Safety, Facial Areas) with multiple items each. Homepage/treatment pages use smaller accordion sections for product details.
blocks.notes: FAQ page is the primary consumer with ~30 accordion items grouped by topic. Source uses emu-accordion component. Each item has a heading (question) and rich-text body (answer with paragraphs, lists, sup references).

blocks.description: Sliding content carousel used for treatment area cards on homepage (Lips, Eyes, Restore, etc.) and product info slides on treatment pages. Each slide typically contains an image, heading, and descriptive text.
blocks.notes: Homepage carousel shows 5 treatment area cards with navigation dots. Treatment pages use carousel for product variants (VOLBELLA, VOLIFT, ULTRA etc). Source uses emu-carousel component.

blocks.description: Grid of content cards showing treatment benefits or features. Homepage uses cards for 'Why JUVÉDERM?' section (natural look, leading brand, lasting results, satisfaction). Treatment pages use cards for step-by-step treatment journey.
blocks.notes: Source site renders these as styled containers with icon/image + heading + text. Can also be used for the treatment journey steps section (Consultation → Treatment → Results).

blocks.description: Tabbed content sections used on treatment pages to show product range details (different JUVÉDERM products). Each tab contains product name, description, and usage info.
blocks.notes: Treatment pages show tabs for JUVÉDERM product variants (VOLBELLA, VOLIFT, ULTRA 4, ULTRA 3, ULTRA 2). Content per tab is relatively simple: heading + paragraph + optional superscript references.

blocks.description: Video embed for the homepage hero background video. Source site uses an auto-playing, looping background video in the hero teaser.
blocks.notes: Homepage hero has a video element. May be handled as a hero variant (hero with video background) rather than a standalone video block. Evaluate whether to use auto-blocked embed or dedicated video block.

blocks.description: Sticky horizontal anchor navigation for FAQ page sections. Shows topic labels (About Juvéderm, Preparation, Consultation, Results, Safety, Facial Areas) that scroll to corresponding accordion groups.
blocks.notes: Source uses juvederm-topicmenu class with sticky positioning. Only used on FAQ page. Could be implemented as a simple custom block with anchor links, or as a section style on an unordered list of anchor links.

blocks.description: Interactive clinic search with location input (postcode/city), Google Maps integration, and results list. Includes search form, map display, and clinic result cards with name/address/phone.
blocks.notes: This is the most complex interactive component. Source uses emu-location-services with Google Maps API. Full-page version on /find-a-clinic. Compact version appears in footer area of homepage/treatment pages. Will require custom JS and external API integration. Consider iframe embed as alternative.

blocks.description: Site footer with 'Vind je kliniek' CTA, social links (Instagram, Facebook), legal/regulatory links, AbbVie/Allergan branding, privacy links, and cookie preferences.
blocks.notes: Auto-loaded by EDS from /footer document. Contains CTA button, social media icons, navigation links, regulatory text, and copyright. Source uses experience fragment with juvederm-footer class.

blocks.description: Two-column or multi-column layout for image + text side-by-side sections. Used for treatment benefit sections with image on one side and descriptive text on the other.
blocks.notes: The boilerplate includes a columns block. Useful for the image+text paired sections that appear throughout homepage and treatment pages (e.g., 'Why JUVÉDERM?' benefits with icons).

blocks.description: Cookie consent/preferences overlay with categories: Required, Functional, Advertising cookies. Shows vendor list and toggle controls.
blocks.notes: Source uses OneTrust-based cookie consent. In EDS, this is typically handled via delayed.js integration with a consent management platform. Not a content block per se - more of a script integration.

defaultContent.description: Standard text content: headings (h1-h5), paragraphs, bulleted/numbered lists, bold/italic text, superscript references, mailto/external links. Used for legal disclaimer text, contact info, product descriptions, and regulatory footnotes.
defaultContent.notes: Legal page is almost entirely default content. Contact page is title + address block. Treatment pages have introductory text sections. All pages have regulatory footnote sections at bottom.

defaultContent.description: CTA buttons linking to clinic finder ('/nl/find-a-clinic') and treatment pages. Primary CTA pattern: 'Vind je kliniek' (Find your clinic). Secondary CTAs link to treatment detail pages.
defaultContent.notes:
In EDS, links wrapped in

become primary buttons,

become secondary. The 'Vind je kliniek' CTA is the global primary action, appearing in header, footer, and inline across pages.

defaultContent.description: Standalone images used as section dividers, decorative elements, or inline illustrations. Source uses Adobe Dynamic Media for responsive image delivery.
defaultContent.notes: Images from source DAM (/content/dam/juvederm-ous/) need to be downloaded and re-hosted. EDS handles responsive image optimization automatically via aem.live CDN.

autoGenerated.description: Site header with logo, dropdown navigation (Behandeling → 5 treatment links, FAQ → 5 anchor links), hamburger menu for mobile, and 'Vind je kliniek' CTA button.
autoGenerated.notes: Auto-loaded by EDS from /nav document. Source uses experience fragment with desktop nav + mobile modal. Navigation has 2 top-level items: Behandeling (treatments) and FAQ, each with 5 sub-items.

autoGenerated.description: Page metadata: title, description, og:title, og:description, og:image, twitter cards, keywords. Managed via metadata block in document.
autoGenerated.notes: Source has comprehensive OG/Twitter metadata. Template is 'content-page' for all pages. Keywords include hyaluronic acid, facial fillers, juvederm, etc.
